texto: Some upstarts always try to get closer to the source of creation by ascending to the source's level. The story of Icarus is of course a parable about the folly of such an effort. Get too close to the sun and your hubris will get you burned. Yet in the eyes of twenty-first-century capitalist culture, which worships at the twin altars of the individual and technology, Icarus had initiative. And his melted wings do not represent some deep character flaw; he just needed better beta testers.
explicacao: Citação de Marcus Wohlsen sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Some upstarts always try to get closer to the source of creation by ascending to the source's lev…” articula sucesso, icarus, progress em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como termino e progress.
